passage.title: Toy Shelf Labels
passage.text: Our group keeps a door basket next to the shoe rack. Before bed, Mina checks it before homework time. The door basket has labels for blocks, cars, and stuffed animals for the day's job. Mom asks us to put each thing in the right place. Sometimes we compare today's label with yesterday's label and notice one small change. We like the door basket because it helps us keep our home routine clear.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the door basket is used.
The first sentence says the door basket is next to the shoe rack.

questions.prompt: When does Mina use the door basket?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Mina uses it before bed.

questions.prompt: What does the door basket have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the door basket has toy labels.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
